perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information may be used only for the following purposes (“Purpose”) : a. evaluation for the issuance of policies of credit insurance or bonding or issuance of an EH grade to the Company; b. evaluation for the issuance of policies of credit insurance or bonding or issuance of an EH grade to Euler Hermes’ customers; and/or c. evaluation of other new or existing credit insurance, bonding , grade , or other insurance business lines carried out by Euler Hermes or its Affiliates;
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information may be used for evaluation for the issuance of policies of credit insurance or bonding.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the explicitly defined scope of 'Purpose', this section now allows for broader interpretation, potentially conflicting with later clauses (like paragraph 1) that restrict the use of Confidential Information to the originally defined 'Purpose'. This could lead to disputes about whether a specific use of the information falls within the permitted scope, as the definition is now incomplete.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Euler Hermes shall keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information only to its Affiliates , employees, contractors , or consultants for the Purpose described above and no other purpose. Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any Affiliate, employee, contractor , or consultant.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the 'Purpose' of using Confidential Information with specific limitations. The changed text omits these limitations, allowing for a broader interpretation of 'Purpose'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which explicitly restricts the use of Confidential Information to the 'Purpose described above and no other purpose'. By removing the defined scope, the changed text creates ambiguity and a conflict regarding the permissible uses of the information, fulfilling the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS “Affiliates” shall mean any person or entity directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common c ontrol with Euler Hermes, where control means the legal power to direct or cause the direction of the general mana gement of the c ompany, partnership , or other legal entity;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS “Affiliates” shall mean any person or entity directly or indirectly related to Euler Hermes.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Affiliates' has been broadened and made ambiguous by removing the specific control criteria. This broader definition clashes with paragraph 1, which holds Euler Hermes liable for breaches by 'Affiliates'. The original, tighter definition limited liability to entities under Euler Hermes' direct or indirect control. The new definition could extend liability to any entity loosely related, creating uncertainty and contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Euler Hermes shall keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information only to its Affiliates , employees, contractors , or consultants for the Purpose described above and no other purpose. Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any Affiliate, employee, contractor , or consultant.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Affiliates' with specific control criteria, limiting Euler Hermes' liability for their breaches in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text broadens the definition of 'Affiliates' to any entity 'directly or indirectly related,' removing the control criteria. This creates a contradiction because Euler Hermes is now potentially liable for the actions of entities over which it has no control, conflicting with the implicit limitation of liability in the original definition, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes shall protect the Confidential Information from disclos ure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above, by using the same degree of care, but no less than reasonable care, that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature .
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes shall protect the Confidential Information from disclosure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'reasonable care' standard creates a contradiction. While Euler Hermes is obligated to protect Confidential Information as authorized in paragraph 1, there's no longer a specified level of care required. This could be interpreted as any level of protection is sufficient, even negligence, directly contradicting the intent of the original clause which mandated a minimum level of protection ('no less than reasonable care').
perturbation.contradicted_text: Euler Hermes shall protect the Confidential Information from disclos ure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that Euler Hermes must protect Confidential Information with 'no less than reasonable care.' The changed text removes this standard of care. The contradicted text states that Euler Hermes shall protect the Confidential Information. By removing the 'reasonable care' standard, the changed text introduces a contradiction because without a specified level of care, any level of protection, even gross negligence, could arguably satisfy the obligation to 'protect' the information, directly contradicting the implicit intent of providing adequate protection indicated in the original text. Although the text is similar in both the Changed and Contradicted text, the omission of the level of care creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes shall promptly return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession but may retain copies of any and all notes, analyses , references, or other material prepared by Euler Hermes that inc orporates any of the Confidential Information , which shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement , nothwithstanding Paragraph 10 of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes shall promptly return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause allowing Euler Hermes to retain copies of notes/analyses while maintaining confidentiality, it contradicts the implicit right to analyze the information for the stated 'Purpose'. If Euler Hermes cannot retain any analysis, it significantly hinders its ability to use the Confidential Information for the agreed purposes, therefore contradicting the contract's primary intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS , the Company has expressed a willingness to furnish to Euler Hermes and its Affiliates certain confidential financial statements and other financ ial information relating to the affairs of the Company , whether submitted in oral, written, magnetic, electronic , or other form and regardless of whether expressly identified as confidential (“Confidential Information”) . The Confidential Information may be used only for the following purposes (“Purpose”) : a. evaluation for the issuance of policies of credit insurance or bonding or issuance of an EH grade to the Company; b. evaluation for the issuance of policies of credit insurance or bonding or issuance of an EH grade to Euler Hermes’ customers; and/or c. evaluation of other new or existing credit insurance, bonding , grade , or other insurance business lines carried out by Euler Hermes or its Affiliates;
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed Euler Hermes to retain copies of analyses while maintaining confidentiality. The changed text removes this clause, preventing Euler Hermes from retaining any analysis of the Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the 'Purpose' clause, which explicitly states that the Confidential Information is to be used for evaluation for insurance policies, bonding, or EH grades, as effective evaluation necessitates analysis. By removing the ability to retain any analysis, the changed text creates a strong contradiction with the stated purpose of the agreement, hindering Euler Hermes's ability to fulfill its obligations, and creating an internal conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes acknowledges that monet ary damages for improper use or disclosure of Confidential Information may be inadequate. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, in addition to other available remedies, Company is entitled to seek equitable relief, including inj unctive relief, without the need to post a bond
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes acknowledges that monet ary damages for improper use or disclosure of Confidential Information may be inadequate. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, in addition to other available remedies, Company is entitled to seek equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'without the need to post a bond' from the equitable relief clause creates an inconsistency. The original text explicitly waived the bond requirement. The modified text implies that a bond *may* be required, which contradicts the original intention of providing easier access to injunctive relief. This introduces uncertainty and potential legal challenges regarding whether a bond is necessary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Euler Hermes acknowledges that monet ary damages for improper use or disclosure of Confidential Information may be inadequate. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, in addition to other available remedies, Company is entitled to seek equitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'Company is entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctive relief, without the need to post a bond.' The changed text removes 'without the need to post a bond,' implying that a bond *may* be required. Since the Contradicted text is equal to the Changed Text, the changed text causes a contradiction because now a bond could be required when the original text said it wasn't required.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall expire at the end of five (5) years from the date set forth below , and may be renewed upon mutual written consent of Euler Hermes and the Company .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall expire at the end of five (5) years from the date set forth below.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the option for renewal creates a potential contradiction. While the agreement initially defines the terms for the use and protection of confidential information, eliminating the renewal clause implies that after five years, there is no longer any obligation to protect said information, even if it's still being used by Euler Hermes for the purposes outlined in the agreement (evaluation for insurance, bonding, etc.). This contradicts the initial intent of maintaining confidentiality and potentially allows for misuse of confidential data after the initial term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Euler Hermes shall keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information only to its Affiliates , employees, contractors , or consultants for the Purpose described above and no other purpose. Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any Affiliate, employee, contractor , or consultant.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for renewal of the agreement, implying continued obligations for confidentiality. The changed text removes the renewal option, potentially releasing Euler Hermes from confidentiality obligations after five years. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states Euler Hermes 'shall keep the Confidential Information confidential'. Without a renewal clause, the duration of this obligation becomes unclear and contradicts the absolute 'shall keep' requirement.
